sections.label: Section 1630b
sections.translation: Yes , for I am in my right mind .

sections.label: Section 1631
sections.original: οὐκ ἔμοιγʼ , εἰ μή μʼ ἐάσεις —
sections.translation: Not in my opinion , if you will not let me —

sections.label: Section 1631b
sections.translation: No , I will not let you !

sections.label: Section 1632
sections.translation: Let me kill my most wicked sister —

sections.label: Section 1632b
sections.translation: No , she is most pious .

sections.label: Section 1633
sections.translation: Who betrayed me —

sections.label: Section 1633b
sections.translation: It was a noble betrayal , correctly done .

sections.label: Section 1634
sections.translation: When she gave my bride to another .

sections.label: Section 1634b
sections.translation: To the one who had a better right .

sections.label: Section 1635
sections.translation: Who has rights over my property ?

sections.label: Section 1635b
sections.translation: The one who received her from her father .

sections.label: Section 1636
sections.translation: But fortune gave her to me .

sections.label: Section 1636b
sections.translation: And necessity took her away .

sections.label: Section 1637
sections.translation: It is not for you to judge my affairs .

sections.label: Section 1637b
sections.translation: Yes , if my counsel is better .

sections.label: Section 1638
sections.translation: So I am your subject , not your ruler .

sections.label: Section 1638b
sections.original: ὅσια δρᾶν , τὰ δʼ ἔκδικʼ οὔ .
sections.translation: Subject to do right , not wrong .

sections.label: Section 1639
sections.translation: It seems you desire to be killed .

sections.label: Section 1639b
sections.translation: Kill me ;

sections.label: Section 1640
sections.original: οὐ κτενεῖς ἡμῶν ἑκόντων , ἀλλʼ ἔμε · ὡς πρὸ δεσποτῶν
sections.translation: you will not kill your sister with my consent , but me instead ; to die for their masters is the most glorious act for noble slaves .

sections.label: Section 1642
sections.original: ἐπίσχες ὀργὰς αἷσιν οὐκ ὀρθῶς φέρῃ ,
sections.translation: Restrain the anger that is wrongly carrying you away , Theoklymenos , king of this land . We , the twin sons of Zeus , are calling you ; Leda once gave birth to us ,

sections.label: Section 1645
sections.original: ἔτικτεν Ἑλένην θʼ , ἣ πέφευγε σοὺς δόμους ·
sections.translation: with Helen , who has fled from your home . For you are angry about a marriage that is not destined for you ; and your sister Theonoe , daughter of a Nereid goddess , does not wrong you when she honors the word of the gods and her father ’ s just commands .

sections.label: Section 1650
sections.original: ἐς μὲν γὰρ αἰεὶ τὸν παρόντα νῦν χρόνον
sections.translation: For it was ordained that Helen should live in your house up to the present time ; but no longer , since Troy is wholly destroyed and she has provided her name to the gods ; she must be united in her own marriage ,

sections.label: Section 1655
sections.original: ἐλθεῖν τʼ ἐς οἴκους καὶ συνοικῆσαι πόσει .
sections.translation: and come home and live with her husband . But hold your black sword away from your sister , and believe that she is acting with discretion in this matter . Long ago , before this , we would have saved our sister , seeing that Zeus has made us gods ;

sections.label: Section 1660
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἥσσονʼ ἦμεν τοῦ πεπρωμένου θʼ ἅμα
sections.translation: but we are weaker than fate and also than the gods , who decreed these things to happen in this way .

sections.label: Section 1662
sections.original: σοὶ μὲν τάδʼ αὐδῶ , συγγόνῳ δʼ ἐμῇ λέγω ·
sections.translation: This is my bidding to you , while I say to my sister : Sail on with your husband ; and you shall have a favorable breeze ; for we , your two savior brothers ,

sections.label: Section 1665
sections.translation: riding over the sea , will send you to your fatherland . And when you make the last turn of the race-course and end your life , you will be named as a goddess , and share libations with the Dioskouroi , and receive gifts from men with us ; for such is the will of Zeus .

sections.label: Section 1670
sections.original: οὗ δʼ ὥρισέν σοι πρῶτα Μαιάδος τόκος ,
sections.translation: And the place where the son of Maia first set the boundary to your course through the air , when he took you away from Sparta , stealing your body so that Paris would not marry you — I mean the island stretched like a sentinel along the coast of Attica — shall be called by your name among men for the future , since it welcomed you

sections.label: Section 1675
sections.original: ἐπεὶ κλοπαίαν σʼ ἐκ δόμων ἐδέξατο .
sections.translation: when you were stolen from your home . And it is destined by the gods that the wanderer Menelaos will dwell in the islands of the blessed ; for deities do not hate the well-born , but the sufferings of the multitude are greater .

sections.label: Section 1680
sections.original: ὦ παῖδε Λήδας καὶ Διός , τὰ μὲν πάρος
sections.translation: You sons of Leda and Zeus , I will let go my former quarrel over your sister ; and mine I shall no longer try to kill . Let Helen go home , if the gods think it right . Know that you are born from the same blood as a sister

sections.label: Section 1685
sections.translation: who is the best and also most self-controlled ; may you fare well , for the sake of Helen ’ s most noble mind , a quality not to be found in many women .

sections.label: Section 1688
sections.translation: Many are the forms of divinities , and many things the gods bring to pass unhoped for .

sections.label: Section 1690
sections.translation: And what was expected has not been fulfilled ; for what was not expected , a god finds a way . Such was the result of this action .
